macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Strong U.S. retail sales keep risk appetite supported, while Fed hike talk grows
items.summary: Markets were buoyed by solid U.S. retail sales, with the control-group measure also holding up, suggesting consumer spending remained resilient. At the same time, hawkish comments from Fed officials and firmer rate expectations pushed Treasury yields higher and added to speculation the Fed could eventually shift toward tightening.

items.title: U.S. equities continue record run as investors weigh inflation and rates
items.summary: The S&P 500 rose 0.8% and continued setting new highs, extending a rally that has left the index up roughly 10% year to date. The move came despite elevated oil prices and renewed concern that higher inflation could keep the Fed cautious.

items.title: U.S. CPI rose 0.6% in April, keeping inflation above the Fed’s target
items.summary: The Bureau of Labor Statistics said the Consumer Price Index increased 0.6% month over month in April and 3.8% over the past 12 months. Energy prices remained a major driver, with gasoline up 28.4% year over year and overall energy up 17.9%.

items.title: Labor market remains firm even as inflation pressures persist
items.summary: Recent BLS data show the U.S. unemployment rate at 4.3% in April and payrolls rising by 115,000. The combination of still-solid employment and sticky inflation supports the view that the Fed can remain restrictive for longer.

items.title: Economic calendar shows key U.S. inflation and jobs releases in focus
items.summary: The latest calendar included U.S. ADP employment, Challenger job cuts, and producer-price data, underscoring a market focus on labor and inflation signals. These reports are shaping expectations for the Fed’s next policy moves.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Senate committee advances U.S. Clarity Act, boosting crypto markets
items.summary: The Senate Banking Committee advanced the Clarity Act, a major crypto market structure bill that would define SEC and CFTC oversight for digital assets. The news helped lift Bitcoin back toward the $81,000 zone and sparked gains across major crypto-related equities including Coinbase, Circle, Strategy, and Robinhood.

items.title: Bitcoin rises above $80,500 as Clarity Act optimism drives sentiment
items.summary: Bitcoin climbed above $80,500 amid renewed optimism over the Clarity Act, though the market remained in a consolidation phase. XRP also outperformed, rising sharply alongside the broader crypto rebound.

items.title: Bitcoin trades between $79,230 and $82,048 in volatile 24-hour range
items.summary: Binance reported Bitcoin moving within a tight but elevated trading range over the past day, reflecting ongoing volatility as traders react to regulatory headlines and broader market positioning. The update also highlighted technical resistance around the 200-day moving average.

items.title: Bitcoin slips back to $81K after rallying to $82K on Clarity Act optimism
items.summary: Bitcoin briefly rallied to around $82,000 before easing back to the $81,000 area as enthusiasm over the Clarity Act cooled slightly. The move underscores how closely crypto prices are tracking U.S. legislative progress.

items.title: CryptoSlate highlights major Clarity Act-driven market move and broader industry updates
items.summary: CryptoSlate reported that crypto markets are rallying on the Clarity Act’s progress, while also flagging other notable industry headlines. The roundup points to continued focus on regulation, Bitcoin price action, and the next wave of project and policy developments.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Fortnite adds Overwatch collab, first-person mode, and new mythic items
items.summary: A new Fortnite update reportedly brings an Overwatch crossover, first-person gameplay, and several mythic items including time-traveling dual pistols and a healing staff. The update also appears to include fresh locations, bug fixes, and hints at an upcoming Ratatouille collaboration.

items.title: Subnautica 2 launches into early access with co-op multiplayer
items.summary: Subnautica 2 has reportedly entered early access, adding online co-op for up to four players. Coverage from creators highlights the sequel’s exploration, base-building, and survival focus as major upgrades to the series formula.

items.title: Hypixel SkyBlock’s Lotus Atoll returns to alpha with major changes
items.summary: Hypixel SkyBlock’s Lotus Atoll has returned to alpha testing with significant changes and improvements. The update suggests the next island is being refined ahead of a broader release.

items.title: NHL playoff action delivers key goals and series-clinching moments
items.summary: Thursday’s playoff slate featured several standout goals, including Mitchell Marner’s between-the-legs opener and Ivan Demidov’s first career playoff goal. The Avalanche also advanced with an overtime winner from Erik Brännström?

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Microsoft reportedly retires Edge’s Copilot Mode as AI features spread across the browser
items.summary: Microsoft is winding down Copilot Mode in Edge and folding AI-related capabilities into the browser more broadly. The move reflects the company’s ongoing push to integrate assistant features into its consumer software stack.

items.title: AMD to bring FSR 4.1 upscaling to older Radeon GPUs
items.summary: AMD says its next-generation FSR 4.1 upscaling technology will extend to older RDNA 3 graphics cards starting in July, broadening access to the feature across a large installed base. The update should help improve image quality and performance in supported games.

items.title: US reportedly allows some Chinese companies to buy NVIDIA H200 AI chips
items.summary: Reports indicate the U.S. has approved sales of NVIDIA’s H200 chips to 10 Chinese companies, though Reuters says no deliveries have occurred yet. The development points to continued sensitivity around advanced chip exports and supply-chain controls.

items.title: Amazon brings Alexa+ to the web
items.summary: Amazon is expanding Alexa+ beyond dedicated devices and onto the web, signaling a broader rollout of its next-generation assistant. The move increases Amazon’s reach in consumer AI-enabled services and direct competition with other major assistants.

items.title: Gartner warns organizations to block AI browsers for now
items.summary: Gartner is recommending that enterprises avoid or block AI browsers, citing concerns over sensitive-data exposure and weakened security controls. The guidance highlights growing enterprise caution around agentic browsing tools.

items.title: Quantum Motion unveils silicon-based quantum computer milestone
items.summary: Quantum Motion has deployed what it says is the first full-stack quantum computer built using standard silicon chip fabrication. The company’s approach could make quantum systems more scalable and manufacturing-friendly over time.
